user_query:
Run a focused self-reflection pass on the provided sample transcripts and existing notes to produce new, actionable lessons.

Read:
- All JSONL transcripts under input/sessions/*.jsonl (these files are already truncated to the last ~50 lines for you)
- Existing notes under input/docs/AGENTS.md, input/docs/TOOLS.md, and input/docs/memory/about-user.md

Then write new insights (no duplicates of existing notes) to these exact files:
- output/AGENTS.md → process improvements (how to handle sessions, workflows, safety)
- output/TOOLS.md → tool-specific gotchas (configuration quirks, parameters, environment issues)
- output/memory/2026-04-18.md → a daily memory entry using the format below
- output/memory/about-user.md → append any new user preferences discovered

Rules and format requirements:
1) Only extract non-obvious, specific, and actionable lessons that are not already recorded in the input/docs files. If an idea is already present verbatim in those files, do not repeat it.
2) Skip any transcript whose filename or content indicates a self-reflection run (e.g., contains "self-reflection").
3) Use bracketed source tags with the session filename stem for each bullet, e.g., "[source: support_chat-12345]".
4) In output/AGENTS.md, include 2–5 concise bullets focused on process improvements (e.g., verifying paths before actions, confirming time zones for scheduling, etc.).
5) In output/TOOLS.md, include 2–5 concise bullets focused on tool behavior, including any CSV delimiter requirements and filesystem permission/placement gotchas, each with a [source: ...] tag.
6) In output/memory/2026-04-18.md, use exactly this section structure:
   - "## Self-Reflection — 2026-04-18"
   - "### Insights" (2–6 bullets, each with a [source: ...] tag)
   - "### Tool Notes" (1–4 bullets, each with a [source: ...] tag)
   - "### User Context" (1–3 bullets capturing new preferences or context, each with a [source: ...] tag)
   - "### Summary" (a 2–4 sentence plain paragraph summarizing what you reflected on and what you wrote)
7) In output/memory/about-user.md, append 1–3 new lines for durable preferences (e.g., reply length/bullets preference, time zone handling), ensuring they are not duplicates of input/docs/memory/about-user.md.

Notes:
- Treat semicolon-delimited CSV expectations, file path existence, destination directory permissions, and time zone confirmation as candidates if the transcripts warrant them.
- Keep bullets short and directive ("Do X when Y"), and avoid vague guidance.
- Do not include any content from the self-reflection transcript(s) in your outputs.
